title: Oman boosts healthcare with AI diagnostics, new hospitals and genome programme under Vision 2040 - Arabian
summary: Oman advances healthcare with AI diagnostics, new hospitals and genome programme under Vision 2040
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
The Oman healthcare sector has undergone a major transformation under Oman Vision 2040, with new hospitals, advanced digital systems and artificial intelligence-driven diagnostics reshaping service delivery and patient outcomes.

The Ministry of Health said the reforms, implemented during the Tenth Five-Year Development Plan, combine infrastructure expansion with legislative and technological upgrades to build a sustainable and inclusive healthcare system.

The sector has been restructured under Royal Decree No. 10/2024, introducing a governance framework based on three specialised units designed to enhance decentralisation and expand the powers of governorates.

Key integration measures include the establishment of the University Medical City and the Medical City for Military and Security Services, alongside the transfer of medical services from the Diwan of Royal Court to the Ministry of Health.

These steps aim to unify healthcare delivery, improve expenditure efficiency and standardise national care systems.

Oman hospitals and facilities

Four major hospitals have been completed and are now operational:

  • A’Suwaiq
  • Khasab
  • Al Mazyounah
  • Wadi Bani Khalid

Expansion projects have also been carried out at Sohar, Nizwa and Sur hospitals, as well as the National Heart Centre.

Primary care capacity has been strengthened with the addition of two health complexes and seven new health centres, alongside upgrades to nine local hospitals.

Specialised services have also been expanded, including the launch of:

  • An IVF centre
  • The National Centre for Virtual Health
  • Nine dialysis units
  • Seven accident and emergency units
  • The Central Public Health Laboratory

New projects under development

Work is ongoing on 21 additional health facilities, with several nearing completion.

Sultan Qaboos Hospital in Salalah has reached a completion rate of 83 per cent, while Samail Hospital stands at 71 per cent completion.

Oman has integrated artificial intelligence into diagnostic services, delivering measurable improvements in efficiency and accuracy.

The National Program for Diabetic Retinopathy Screening has screened more than 30,000 patients, achieving an accuracy rate of 92 per cent while reducing waiting times by more than 80 per cent.

The sector has also launched major digital health initiatives, including the Omani Genome Programme and the Unified Health Record, which connects 255 healthcare facilities across the country.

Oman a regional health hub

These projects are positioning Oman as a regional hub for digital health innovation and advancing the development of personalised medicine.

Efforts to strengthen self-sufficiency have seen the number of operational medical factories rise to 21, with 18 additional projects currently under implementation.

The government has also signed six advance purchase agreements with national manufacturers to localise the production of biopharmaceuticals, intravenous solutions and genetic disease medications.

These measures aim to enhance pharmaceutical sustainability and attract high-quality investment into the sector.

Healthcare reforms have delivered improvements in key performance indicators.

Oman life expectancy

Healthy life expectancy reached 66.07 years in 2023, surpassing the global average, while community satisfaction rose to 82.4 per cent.

Officials said the progress reflects sustained investment in national workforce capabilities and improvements in care quality.

The Ministry of Health said the next phase will focus on improving system efficiency, expanding the use of advanced technologies and strengthening national capabilities to meet population growth.

The long-term goal remains the delivery of a world-class healthcare system aligned with Oman Vision 2040, supporting quality of life and sustainable development.
